 succeeded in 52ms:
\label{rem:no-bifunctor}
\index{bifunctoriality!failure of two-sided}
\index{one-slot obstruction}
Robert-Nicoud--Wierstra~\cite[Section~6]{RNW19} prove that although
the convolution functor $\operatorname{hom}_\alpha(-,-)$ extends to
$\infty_\alpha$-morphisms in either slot separately, there is
\emph{no} honest bifunctor accepting $\infty$-morphisms in both slots
simultaneously. The consequences:
\begin{itemize}
\item Bar-cobar extends to $\infty$-morphisms of chiral algebras (slot~2)
 and to $\infty$-morphisms of chiral coalgebras (slot~1), but not both
 simultaneously.
\item The MC3 categorical lift
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:categorical-cg-all-types}) proceeds one slot at a
 time. The prefundamental CG closure
 (Proposition~\ref{prop:prefundamental-clebsch-gordan}) works at the
 character level ($K_0$) precisely because characters live in one slot;
 the categorical lift requires both.
\item Any claim of two-sided functoriality for bulk/boundary/line
 constructions must be replaced by a homotopy-coherent correspondence
 succeeded in 52ms:
\section{Intrinsic Koszulness: the characterization programme}
\label{sec:concordance-koszulness-programme}
\index{Koszul property!characterization programme|textbf}
\index{chiral Koszulness!intrinsic characterization|textbf}

The PBW criterion (Theorem~\ref{thm:pbw-koszulness-criterion})
detects chiral Koszulness for every standard family but does
not characterize it intrinsically. This section is the
constitutional index for the intrinsic characterization
programme. Full statements and proofs live in the source
chapters cited below.

filtration~$F_\bullet$. The full meta-theorem is
Theorem~\ref{thm:koszul-equivalences-meta} in

 \textbf{AP165 correction (constitutional).}
 The bar complex $\barB(\cA)$ is an $E_1$ coassociative
 coalgebra over $(\mathrm{ChirAss})^!$, the Koszul dual
 cooperad of the chiral associative operad.
 It is \emph{not} an $\SCchtop$-coalgebra.

\paragraph{Chiral Hochschild descent lemma.}
Lemma~\ref{lem:chirhoch-descent} records an explicit step
in the proof of Theorem~H: the chiral Hochschild complex
$\ChirHoch^{\bullet}(\cA)$ arises as the $\Sigma$-coinvariant
descent of the bar complex along Verdier intertwining,
$\ChirHoch^{\bullet}(\cA) \cong
(\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\barB(\cA))_{\Sigma}$,
on the Koszul locus. This identification was implicit in
the original Theorem~H proof; the lemma makes it citable
as a primitive step (A2).

 \textbf{B} & Bar-cobar inversion
- & $\Omegach(\barBch(\cA)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \cA$
- on the Koszul locus
- \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}\textup{)};
- genus~$0$ unconditional; for arbitrary~$\cA$,
- $g \geq 1$ is conditional on
- axiom~\ref{MK:modular}; unconditional on the
- CFT-type standard landscape except integer-spin
- $\beta\gamma$ by Theorem~\ref{thm:pbw-propagation}
- and Remark~\ref{rem:pbw-propagation-scope}
+ & Strict quasi-isomorphism on the Koszul locus
+ $\Omegach(\barBch(\cA)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \cA$
+ \textup{(}Thms~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion},
+ \ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}; off the locus,
+ the counit is an unconditional coderived coacyclic-equivalence,
+ promoted back to an ordinary quasi-isomorphism on collapse loci
  & \ClaimStatusProvedHere \\
 \textbf{C} & Complementarity
- & $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, Z(\cA))$
- \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}\textup{)};
- Verdier-side Lagrangian splitting unconditional;
- ambient shifted-symplectic upgrade conditional on
- perfectness/nondegeneracy
- & \ClaimStatusProvedHere \\
+ & C0: coderived fiber-center identification unconditional,
+ ordinary-derived only on the flat perfect locus
+ \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:fiber-center-identification}\textup{)}.
+ C1: homotopy eigenspace decomposition for all~$g$, with
+ duality/Lagrangian only for $g \geq 1$ and genus~$0$
+ treated separately
+ \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}\textup{)}.
+ C2: shifted-symplectic/BV upgrade conditional on the
+ uniform-weight perfectness package
+ \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:shifted-symplectic-complementarity}\textup{)}
+ & C0/C1: \ClaimStatusProvedHere; C2: \ClaimStatusConditional \\
 \textbf{D} & Modular characteristic
  & $\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA)=\kappa(\cA)\lambda_g$
- for uniform-weight modular Koszul algebras
- \textup{(}Definition~\ref{def:scalar-lane}\textup{)}
- at all genera;
- genus~$1$ universal for all families;
- additivity and duality constraints;
- Faber--Pandharipande free-energy formula
- \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:genus-universality}\textup{)}
+ on the proved uniform-weight lane at all genera
+ \textup{(}Definition~\ref{def:scalar-lane},
+ Thm~\ref{thm:modular-characteristic}\textup{)};
+ genus~$1$ is universal for all families;
+ multi-weight $g \geq 2$ acquires the cross-channel term
+ $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$; the routing
+ $\Theta_\cA \to \mathrm{obs}_g \to$ family index is non-circular
  & \ClaimStatusProvedHere \\
 \textbf{H} & Chiral Hochschild
- & $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ polynomial with Hilbert polynomial
- $P(t)=1+t^2$, Koszul-functorial;
- $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg))\cong\fg$ at generic level
- \textup{(}Thms~\ref{thm:w-algebra-hochschild},
- \ref{thm:virasoro-hochschild},
- \ref{thm:critical-level-cohomology};
+ & On the Koszul locus at generic level:
+ $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ is concentrated in $\{0,1,2\}$,
+ satisfies the duality shift~$[2]$, has degree-$\leq 2$
+ Hilbert polynomial, and obeys
+ $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg))\cong\fg$ for affine KM;
+ the collision-depth spectral sequence collapses by local
+ Fulton--MacPherson formality
+ \textup{(}Thms~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
+ \ref{thm:hochschild-polynomial-growth};
  Prop.~\ref{prop:chirhoch1-affine-km}\textup{)}
  & \ClaimStatusProvedHere
 \end{tabular}
@@ -89,6 +97,13 @@
 MC element $\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\gAmod)$ exists by the bar-intrinsic
 construction (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}:
 $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - d_0$ is MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$).
+This is the coinvariant carrier for the main theorem.
+The ordered lift
+$\Theta_\cA^{E_1} \in \MC((\gAmod)^{E_1})$
+is constructed separately in the $\Eone$-modular chapter
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-mc-element}\textup{)},
+and averaging recovers the concordance-level class
+$\Theta_\cA$.
 The shadow obstruction tower
 $\Theta_\cA^{\le 2} \to \Theta_\cA^{\le 3} \to \Theta_\cA^{\le 4}
 \to \cdots$ consists of its finite-order projections.

3035:\item \emph{Critical level}: $V_{-h^\vee}(\fg)$ is uncurved ($\kappa=0$), self-dual under the Feigin--Frenkel involution ($k=-h^\vee$ is a fixed point of $k \mapsto -k-2h^\vee$), center~$= \mathfrak{z}(\widehat{\fg})$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:critical-level-cohomology}).

4737:Index & GRR: scalar genus series $= \kappa(\cA) \cdot (\hat{A}(ix) - 1)$; genus-$1$ unconditional & \textbf{Proved} & Thm~\ref{thm:family-index} \\[2pt]
4741:$H$ & Chiral Hochschild: $\ChirHoch^n = 0$ for $n > 2$, $P(t) = 1+t^2$; & \textbf{Proved} (generic level, & Thms~\ref{thm:w-algebra-hochschild}, \\

 succeeded in 51ms:
 $H^*(\barB^{(g)}(\cA), \Dg{g})$ and its variation over
 $\mathcal{M}_g$, is categorically richer
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:chain-modular-functor}).

\item \emph{Deformation-obstruction complementarity
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}).}
 For a Koszul pair $(\cA, \cA^!)$, the genus-$g$ obstructions
 satisfy $\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) + \mathrm{obs}_g(\cA^!) =
 [\text{characteristic class of } Z(\cA)]$ in $H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g)$.
 The Kodaira--Spencer map is constructed for all Koszul pairs
 and all genera (Theorem~\ref{thm:kodaira-spencer-chiral-complete}).

\item \emph{$\Eone$-chiral Koszul duality
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-chiral-koszul-duality}).}
 The bar-cobar adjunction extends to $\Eone$-chiral algebras
 (nonlocal vertex algebras with $R$-matrix braiding), covering
 Yangians and toroidal algebras, beyond the
 $\Einf$ (commutative/local) setting of BD and FG.

\item \emph{Explicit computations.}

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '4568,4610p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
Five bridges connect the two volumes:

\begin{proposition}[Bar-cobar bridge; \ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere]
\label{prop:vol2-bar-cobar-bridge}%
The $\mathrm{SC}^{\mathrm{ch,top}}$ bar-cobar Quillen equivalence of
Volume~II recovers the chiral bar-cobar adjunction of Theorem~A
(Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}) for boundary
operators of the 3d theory. The closed color specializes to the BD
chiral operad (Vol~II, associated graded proposition).
\end{proposition}

\begin{conjecture}[Hochschild bridge; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
\label{conj:vol2-hochschild-bridge}
The bulk algebra of the 3d theory is the derived center
$Z_{\mathrm{der}}(\cA^!)$, which identifies with the chiral
Hochschild cochain complex $\operatorname{HH}^*_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA^!)$
via the corrected Koszul triangle
(Vol~II, Theorem~J): $A_{\mathrm{bulk}} \simeq
Z_{\mathrm{der}}(B_\partial) \simeq
\operatorname{HH}^*_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA^!)$,

codex
The MC3 lane is the most drifted part of the concordance: the live source no longer uses the old “type A unconditional / other types via Lemma L” split. I’m checking the exact theorem text before I rewrite those summaries, because the concordance needs to advertise the proved evaluation-core theorem and the Baxter-constrained type-A post-core route, not the superseded conjectural packaging.

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '4128,4178p' chapters/examples/yangians_computations.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:

\begin{corollary}[Three-layer MC3 status after categorical CG closure;
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{cor:mc3-all-types}%
\index{MC3!all simple types|textbf}%
\index{MC3!three-layer split}
MC3 splits into three layers with the following status.
\begin{itemize}
\item \emph{Layer~1 \textup{(}MC3a, all simple types,
 unconditional\textup{)}}: the categorical Clebsch--Gordan closure
 for prefundamental modules of every simple Lie algebra
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:categorical-cg-all-types}}\textup{)}.
\item \emph{Layer~2 \textup{(}MC3b, all simple types,
 unconditional\textup{)}}: the DK-2/3 bar-cobar equivalence on the
 evaluation-generated core
 \textup{(}Corollary~\textup{\ref{cor:dk23-all-types}}\textup{)}.
\item \emph{Layer~3 \textup{(}MC3c, shifted-envelope generation
 inside~$\widehat{D}(\mathcal{O}^{\mathrm{sh}}_{\leq 0})$\textup{)}}:
 unconditional in type~$A$
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:shifted-prefundamental-generation}}\textup{)},

  & \ClaimStatusProvedHere \\
 \textbf{B} & Bar-cobar inversion
- & $\Omegach(\barBch(\cA)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \cA$
- on the Koszul locus
- \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}\textup{)};
- genus~$0$ unconditional; for arbitrary~$\cA$,
- $g \geq 1$ is conditional on
- axiom~\ref{MK:modular}; unconditional on the
- CFT-type standard landscape except integer-spin
- $\beta\gamma$ by Theorem~\ref{thm:pbw-propagation}
- and Remark~\ref{rem:pbw-propagation-scope}
+ & Strict quasi-isomorphism on the Koszul locus
+ $\Omegach(\barBch(\cA)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \cA$
+ \textup{(}Thms~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion},
+ \ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}; off the locus,
+ the counit is an unconditional coderived coacyclic-equivalence,
+ promoted back to an ordinary quasi-isomorphism on collapse loci
  & \ClaimStatusProvedHere \\
 \textbf{C} & Complementarity
- & $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, Z(\cA))$
- \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}\textup{)};
- Verdier-side Lagrangian splitting unconditional;
- ambient shifted-symplectic upgrade conditional on
- perfectness/nondegeneracy
- & \ClaimStatusProvedHere \\
+ & C0: coderived fiber-center identification unconditional,
+ ordinary-derived only on the flat perfect locus
+ \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:fiber-center-identification}\textup{)}.
+ C1: homotopy eigenspace decomposition for all~$g$, with
+ duality/Lagrangian only for $g \geq 1$ and genus~$0$
+ treated separately
+ \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}\textup{)}.
+ C2: shifted-symplectic/BV upgrade conditional on the
+ uniform-weight perfectness package
+ \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:shifted-symplectic-complementarity}\textup{)}
+ & C0/C1: \ClaimStatusProvedHere; C2: \ClaimStatusConditional \\
 \textbf{D} & Modular characteristic
  & $\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA)=\kappa(\cA)\lambda_g$
- for uniform-weight modular Koszul algebras
- \textup{(}Definition~\ref{def:scalar-lane}\textup{)}
- at all genera;
- genus~$1$ universal for all families;
- additivity and duality constraints;
- Faber--Pandharipande free-energy formula
- \textup{(}Thm~\ref{thm:genus-universality}\textup{)}
+ on the proved uniform-weight lane at all genera
+ \textup{(}Definition~\ref{def:scalar-lane},
+ Thm~\ref{thm:modular-characteristic}\textup{)};
+ genus~$1$ is universal for all families;
+ multi-weight $g \geq 2$ acquires the cross-channel term
+ $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$; the routing
+ $\Theta_\cA \to \mathrm{obs}_g \to$ family index is non-circular
  & \ClaimStatusProvedHere \\
 \textbf{H} & Chiral Hochschild
- & $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ polynomial with Hilbert polynomial
- $P(t)=1+t^2$, Koszul-functorial;
- $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg))\cong\fg$ at generic level
- \textup{(}Thms~\ref{thm:w-algebra-hochschild},
- \ref{thm:virasoro-hochschild},
- \ref{thm:critical-level-cohomology};
+ & On the Koszul locus at generic level:
+ $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ is concentrated in $\{0,1,2\}$,
+ satisfies the duality shift~$[2]$, has degree-$\leq 2$
+ Hilbert polynomial, and obeys
+ $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg))\cong\fg$ for affine KM;
+ the collision-depth spectral sequence collapses by local
+ Fulton--MacPherson formality
+ \textup{(}Thms~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
+ \ref{thm:hochschild-polynomial-growth};
  Prop.~\ref{prop:chirhoch1-affine-km}\textup{)}
  & \ClaimStatusProvedHere
 \end{tabular}
@@ -89,6 +97,13 @@
 MC element $\Theta_\cA \in \MC(\gAmod)$ exists by the bar-intrinsic
 construction (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}:
 $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - d_0$ is MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$).
+This is the coinvariant carrier for the main theorem.
+The ordered lift
+$\Theta_\cA^{E_1} \in \MC((\gAmod)^{E_1})$
+is constructed separately in the $\Eone$-modular chapter
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-mc-element}\textup{)},
+and averaging recovers the concordance-level class
+$\Theta_\cA$.
 The shadow obstruction tower
 $\Theta_\cA^{\le 2} \to \Theta_\cA^{\le 3} \to \Theta_\cA^{\le 4}
 \to \cdots$ consists of its finite-order projections.


thinking
**Evaluating theorem updates**

I’m considering how to handle the post-core route. It seems wiser to avoid outdated references like `cor:mc3-all-types` and focus on the current type-A reduction theorem. Since the user mentioned the MC3 update, I need to adjust the concordance accordingly.

Although the corollary exists, it's superseded. I should possibly touch on `cor:mc3-all-types` as historical, but I need to prioritize the user's current rectification session, especially regarding citing `thm:mc3-type-a-resolution` over older materials.
codex
I’m rewriting the duplicated theorem summaries now. The goal is not cosmetic consistency; it’s to stop the concordance from advertising theorem surfaces that the live source no longer claims, especially the old MC3 layer split and the old “critical level belongs to Theorem H” shorthand.

+ with the derived center in the coderived category unconditionally,
+ and in the ordinary derived category on the flat perfect locus
+ \textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:fiber-center-identification}\textup{)}.
+ C1 gives the homotopy eigenspace decomposition for all genera, with
+ duality and the Lagrangian package for $g \geq 1$, while genus~$0$
+ is treated separately
+ \textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}\textup{)}.
+ C2 is the shifted-symplectic/BV upgrade, conditional on the

 \item \emph{Chiral Hochschild cohomology
- (Theorems~\ref{thm:w-algebra-hochschild},
- \ref{thm:virasoro-hochschild},
- \ref{thm:critical-level-cohomology};
+ (Theorems~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
+ \ref{thm:hochschild-polynomial-growth};
  Proposition~\ref{prop:chirhoch1-affine-km}).}
- For the principal $\Walg$-algebra $\Walg^k(\fg)$ at generic level:
- $\ChirHoch^n(\Walg^k(\fg)) = 0$ for $n > 2$, with Hilbert polynomial
- $P(t) = 1 + t^2$ (concentration in degrees $\{0,1,2\}$;
- the continuous Lie algebra cohomology
- $H^*_{\mathrm{cont}} = \mathbb{C}[\Theta_1, \ldots, \Theta_r]$
- is a different, unbounded invariant).
- Rank~$1$ (Virasoro): $P(t) = 1 + t^2$.
- Rank $r \geq 2$: $P(t) = 1 + t^2$ at generic level.
+ On the Koszul locus at generic level, the collision-depth spectral
+ sequence for the Fulton--MacPherson stratification collapses at
+ $E_2=E_\infty$ because each local
+ $\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$ is formal. Hence $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ is
+ concentrated in cohomological degrees $\{0,1,2\}$, carries the
+ derived duality shift~$[2]$, and has Hilbert polynomial of degree at
+ most~$2$.
+ For the principal $\Walg$ and Virasoro lanes this polynomial is
+ $1+t^2$ at generic level.
  For affine Kac--Moody at generic level:
  $\ChirHoch^1(V_k(\fg)) \cong \fg$
- (Proposition~\ref{prop:chirhoch1-affine-km}).
- At critical level $k = \critLevel$:
- exterior$\otimes$polynomial structure
- $\Lambda^*(P_1, \ldots, P_r) \otimes \mathbb{C}[\Theta_1, \ldots, \Theta_r]$
- (Fuks--Feigin--Tsygan).
- Functorial under Koszul duality via the Connes
- operator~$S$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
- Corollary~\ref{cor:hochschild-cup-exchange}).
+ \textup{(}Proposition~\ref{prop:chirhoch1-affine-km}\textup{)}.
+ Critical level is separate:
+ Theorem~H does not apply there, and the oper/Feigin--Frenkel package
+ is recorded instead by Theorem~\ref{thm:critical-level-cohomology}.
+ Functoriality under Koszul duality passes through the Connes
+ operator~$S$
+ \textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
+ Corollary~\ref{cor:hochschild-cup-exchange}\textup{)}.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '236,254p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
formal-disk restriction, but the embedding is not an
equivalence: vertex algebras live on the formal disk~$D$,
not on a bare point; the deformation retract
$\mathbb{A}^1 \to \mathrm{pt}$ is additional data
(specifying it and the attendant homotopy transfer is part
of the comparison); and
on~$\mathbb{P}^1$ the global topology of FM
compactifications introduces Arnold relations absent over
a point.
The Drinfeld--Kohno bridge recovers the classical $R$-matrix
as the genus-$0$ binary shadow projection
(Construction~\ref{constr:dk-shadow-projections};
DK-0/1 proved, DK-2/3 proved on the evaluation-generated core,
DK-4/5 conjectural).
The Feigin--Frenkel center
$H^0(\barB(\widehat{\fg}_{\critLevel})) \cong
\operatorname{Fun}(\operatorname{Op}_{\fg^\vee}(D))$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:oper-bar-h0-dl}) is the bar \emph{cohomology}
at the critical level $k = \critLevel$, where $\kappa = 0$

- (Fuks--Feigin--Tsygan).

+ Critical level is separate:
+ Theorem~H does not apply there, and the oper/Feigin--Frenkel package

+ operator~$S$

+ Corollary~\ref{cor:hochschild-cup-exchange}\textup{)}.

MC1 & PBW filtration & Genus-$0$ bar complex is formal:
 $E_2$-degeneration gives $H^*(\barB(\cA)) \cong$ quadratic dual
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:pbw-koszulness-criterion}).
 Holds unconditionally for universal algebras
 (Proposition~\ref{prop:pbw-universality}).
 For simple quotients $L_k(\fg)$ at admissible levels with
 denominator $q \geq 3$ and $\mathrm{rk}(\fg) \geq 2$,
 PBW concentration \emph{fails}: the Cartan subalgebra produces
 $\mathrm{rk}(\fg)$-dimensional bar cohomology in~$H^2$
 (Conjecture~\ref{conj:admissible-koszul-rank-obstruction}) \\
MC2 & $D^2 = 0$ & Bar-intrinsic MC element: $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - d_0$
 is automatically MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}).
 No homotopy correction needed \\
MC3 & Prefundamental CG & \textbf{Proved for all simple types on the evaluation-generated core}.
 All-types package~(i) is proved
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:categorical-cg-all-types}),
 and the generated-core DK comparison surface
 \textup{(}traditionally packaged as DK-2/3\textup{)}
 on the evaluation-generated core is proved separately

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '3310,3338p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
 Key input: multiplicity-free $q$-characters for fundamental reps
 \cite{ChariMoura06} replace the minuscule hypothesis.
 Type~$A$ identifies the remaining post-CG completion packet
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution});
 no global all-types closure is claimed on this row \\
MC4 & Strong filtration & Completion is formal inverse limit: the strong
 filtration axiom $\mu_r(F^{i_1},\ldots,F^{i_r}) \subset
 F^{i_1+\cdots+i_r}$ gives degree cutoff
 (Lemma~\ref{lem:degree-cutoff}), making continuity
 and Mittag-Leffler automatic
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:completed-bar-cobar-strong}) \\
MC5 & Genus tower & Five components:
 (1)~\textbf{Analytic HS-sewing proved} at all genera: polynomial OPE growth $+$ subexponential sector growth implies convergence
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:general-hs-sewing});
 (2)~\textbf{Genus-$0$ algebraic BRST/bar proved}
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:algebraic-string-dictionary});
 (3)~\textbf{$D^{\mathrm{co}}$-level BV=bar proved} for all four shadow classes including class~M
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:bv-bar-coderived});
 (4)~genuswise chain-level BV/BRST/bar identification \textbf{conjectural}
 (class~M chain-level false; Conjecture~\ref{conj:v1-master-bv-brst});

$\mathrm{SC}^{\mathrm{ch,top}}$ is proved in Volume~II
(via Kontsevich formality and transfer from the classical
Swiss--cheese operad), so the bar--cobar adjunction for
$\mathrm{SC}^{\mathrm{ch,top}}$-algebras is a Quillen
\emph{equivalence}. Volume~II has \emph{no remaining conjectural
algebraic inputs}; its only conditional inputs are the
standing analytic hypotheses (H1)--(H4).
Five bridges connect the two volumes:

\begin{proposition}[Bar-cobar bridge; \ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere]
\label{prop:vol2-bar-cobar-bridge}%
The $\mathrm{SC}^{\mathrm{ch,top}}$ bar-cobar Quillen equivalence of
Volume~II recovers the chiral bar-cobar adjunction of Theorem~A
(Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}) for boundary
operators of the 3d theory. The closed color specializes to the BD
chiral operad (Vol~II, associated graded proposition).
\end{proposition}
 succeeded in 51ms:
\begin{remark}[Three-pillar architecture and the MC open problems]
\label{rem:three-pillar-mc-unification}
\index{three-pillar architecture!MC open problems}
\index{MC open problems!three-pillar unification}
The three-pillar architecture determines the MC resolution strategy:
\begin{itemize}
\item \emph{MC1 and MC2}: Pillar~B realizes every algebra as an MC
 element in the modular convolution $sL_\infty$-algebra; the
 finite-order projections $\Theta_\cA^{\leq r}$ (the proved shadow
 obstruction tower) are the constructive content.
 MC1 (PBW concentration) holds unconditionally for universal
 algebras $V_k(\fg)$, $\mathrm{Vir}_c$, $\mathcal{W}^k(\fg)$
 (Proposition~\ref{prop:pbw-universality}). For simple quotients
 $L_k(\fg)$ at admissible levels, PBW concentration fails at
 $\mathrm{rk}(\fg) \geq 2$ with denominator $q \geq 3$: the
 abelian Cartan subalgebra contributes $\mathrm{rk}(\fg)$ classes
 to $H^2(\barB)$
 (Conjecture~\ref{conj:admissible-koszul-rank-obstruction}).
\item \emph{MC3}: MC3 is \textbf{proved} for all simple types on the
evaluation-generated core. The categorical CG decomposition is proved via
 exited 1 in 51ms:

 succeeded in 51ms:
geometric bar complex. The forward direction is
Proposition~\ref{prop:d-module-purity-km}; the new content
is the converse via the Hitchin variation of Hodge structure.

\paragraph{Three-lens identification of the shadow tower.}
Proposition~\ref{prop:shadow-tower-three-lenses} identifies
three equivalent readings of the degree-$r$ shadow
$S_r(\cA)$: algebraic (bar-intrinsic MC projection),
geometric (genus-$0$ transferred $L_\infty$ formality
obstruction), and holographic (boundary-bulk correction
in the derived-centre factorization).

\paragraph{Generating depth versus algebraic depth.}
Definition~\ref{def:generating-depth} formalizes the
distinction mandated: the generating depth
$d_{\mathrm{gen}}(\cA)$ is the smallest degree at which
higher operations are recursively determined, while the
algebraic depth $d_{\mathrm{alg}}(\cA)$ is the smallest
degree beyond which all operations vanish. Virasoro:
$d_{\mathrm{gen}} = 3$ (finite), $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = \infty$

codex
The package-level prose is now aligned with the source. I’m on the last drift layer: status tables and cross-volume bridge summaries. Those are where the same stale claims still reappear as compact labels, especially `B_mod`, the old proof circuit, and the old C/H shorthand.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '3906,3924p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobarexec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '3288,3338p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
coincides with the Virasoro tower at $c = c(M(p,q))$:
class~$\mathbf{M}$ with the standard shadow metric.
At the quotient level, the curved-DG structure introduces
curvature at the degree of the first null vector (level~$pq$
for~$M(p,q)$). The modular $S$-matrix and Verlinde fusion
rules are verified computationally for $M(4,3)$ (Ising, $c = 1/2$),
$M(5,4)$ (tricritical Ising, $c = 7/10$),
and $M(6,5)$ (three-state Potts, $c = 4/5$).
The shadow metric
$Q_L(t) = (2\kappa + 3\alpha t)^2 + 2\Delta\,t^2$
(Definition~\ref{def:shadow-metric}) packages the dichotomy
geometrically: $Q_L$ is a perfect square iff
$\Delta = 0$, and the spectral curve
$\Sigma_L = \{H^2 = t^4 Q_L\}$
(Corollary~\ref{cor:spectral-curve}) is rational.

\paragraph{Ran-coherent bar-cobar equivalence.}
Theorem~\ref{thm:ran-coherent-bar-cobar} resolves the Ran-space
formality gap identified in earlier audits: the bar-cobar
 succeeded in 51ms:

\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.25}
\begin{tabular}{@{}llp{7.5cm}@{}}
\toprule
\textbf{Level} & \textbf{Formality input} & \textbf{Consequence} \\
\midrule
MC1 & PBW filtration & Genus-$0$ bar complex is formal:
 $E_2$-degeneration gives $H^*(\barB(\cA)) \cong$ quadratic dual
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:pbw-koszulness-criterion}).
 Holds unconditionally for universal algebras
 (Proposition~\ref{prop:pbw-universality}).
 For simple quotients $L_k(\fg)$ at admissible levels with
 denominator $q \geq 3$ and $\mathrm{rk}(\fg) \geq 2$,
 PBW concentration \emph{fails}: the Cartan subalgebra produces
 $\mathrm{rk}(\fg)$-dimensional bar cohomology in~$H^2$
 (Conjecture~\ref{conj:admissible-koszul-rank-obstruction}) \\
MC2 & $D^2 = 0$ & Bar-intrinsic MC element: $\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - d_0$
 is automatically MC because $D_\cA^2 = 0$
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}).

differential $D_\cA$ satisfying $D_\cA^2 = 0$ at all genera and
degrees
(Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-modular-operad},
Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-diff-squares-zero}).
This is the foundational consistency: the universal MC element
$\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - d_0$ exists in $\MC(\fg^{\mathrm{mod}}_\cA)$
precisely because $D_\cA^2 = 0$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}).
The test is algebraic: it holds for every chiral algebra satisfying
(H1)--(H4), with no Koszulness hypothesis.

\smallskip
\noindent\textbf{Test~2: $\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) = \kappa(\cA)\cdot\lambda_g$
(obstruction = modular characteristic $\times$ Hodge class).}
The genus-$g$ obstruction class in $H^{2g}(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g)$
factors as a universal algebra-dependent scalar $\kappa(\cA)$ times
the Faber--Pandharipande Hodge integral $\lambda_g$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:modular-characteristic}).
This is proved unconditionally at genus~$1$ for all modular Koszul
algebras, and at all genera on the uniform-weight lane
 succeeded in 51ms:

\subsection{The Lagrangian form of complementarity}

Theorem~C (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}) establishes
the direct-sum decomposition
$Q_g(\cA) \oplus Q_g(\cA^!)
= H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, \mathcal{Z}(\cA))$
via the eigenspace decomposition of the Verdier involution.
The natural strengthening is a Lagrangian-polarization theorem,
now proved in two layers:

\begin{theorem}[Lagrangian complementarity;
\ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:lagrangian-complementarity}
\index{Lagrangian!complementarity|textbf}
For a modular Koszul pair $(\cA, \cA^!)$, the following hold:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item the complex
 $C_g := R\Gamma(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, \mathcal{Z}_{\cA})$
 carries the proved Verdier-side
 succeeded in 52ms:
 & Koszul resolution $+$ chiral endomorphism calculus \\
\ref{prop:ds-chirhoch-compatibility}
 & DS--ChirHoch compatibility:
 DS reduction commutes with chiral Hochschild
 \textup{(}Proposition~\ref{prop:ds-chirhoch-compatibility}\textup{)}
 & BRST complex $+$ HPL transfer \\
\ref{prop:ker-av-schur-weyl}
 & Kernel of averaging: $\dim\ker(\mathrm{av}_n) = n! - \binom{n+d-1}{n}$
 closed formula via Schur--Weyl
 \textup{(}Proposition~\ref{prop:ker-av-schur-weyl}\textup{)}
 & Schur--Weyl duality $+$ symmetric group characters \\
Vol.~III
 & $E_1$-obstruction is categorical:
 $E_1 \not\to E_2$ promotion obstructed by
 non-trivial Drinfeld associator
 \textup{(}Volume~III proposition on the categorical $E_2$-obstruction for CY$_3$\textup{)}
 & associator $+$ monodromy \\
\ref{conj:yangian-ordered-center}
 & Ordered chiral center of the Yangian:
 derived center of $Y_\hbar(\fg)^{\mathrm{ch}}$
 succeeded in 52ms:
genus (MC5, Conjecture~\ref{conj:v1-master-bv-brst}).
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[PVA quantization bridge]
\label{rem:concordance-pva-bridge}
\index{Poisson vertex algebra!quantization bridge}%
Volume~II constructs the modular PVA quantization pipeline:
a Poisson vertex algebra $V$ (the classical shadow of a
chiral algebra) determines a stable-graph modular deformation
complex $\mathrm{Def}_{\mathrm{cyc}}^{\mathrm{mod}}(V)$,
and the genus-$1$ obstruction is the variational modular class
$\mathrm{Mod}(V) = [\Delta_{\mathrm{cyc}} P_\Pi]$.
For the $\mathcal{W}_3$ PVA, $\mathrm{Mod}(V) = 0$
(Volume~II, Theorem~N). The lift freedom is one-dimensional,
generated by the central-parameter direction: the unique
deformation that produces the quantized $\mathcal{W}_3$
at central charge $c = c(k)$. This connects Volume~II's
PVA descent (Theorem~G) with Volume~I's shadow obstruction tower
(the degree-$2$ projection $\Theta_\cA^{\leq 2}$ recovers
the modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$, which is the
 succeeded in 52ms:
 & \S\ref{subsec:concordance-operadic-complexity} \\
Archetype classification
 & \texttt{thm:nms-archetype-trichotomy}
 & App.~\ref{app:nonlinear-modular-shadows} \\
Finite termination
 & \texttt{thm:nms-finite-termination}
 & App.~\ref{app:nonlinear-modular-shadows} \\
Discriminant
 & \texttt{thm:ds-bar-gf-discriminant}
 & Landscape census \\
$\mathcal{N}{=}2$ SCA Koszulness
 & \texttt{prop:n2-koszulness}
 & Ch.~\ref{chap:n2-sca} \\
$\mathcal{N}{=}2$ CE/bar gap
 & \texttt{rem:n2-ce-chiral-gap}
 & Ch.~\ref{chap:n2-sca} \\
$V^\natural$ modular char.
 & \texttt{prop:moonshine-kappa}
 & Ch.~\ref{chap:moonshine} \\

+(Theorems~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
+\ref{thm:hochschild-polynomial-growth}) via dimensional
 descent.
 \end{conjecture}

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '3912,3920p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
$M(5,4)$ (tricritical Ising, $c = 7/10$),
and $M(6,5)$ (three-state Potts, $c = 4/5$).
The shadow metric
$Q_L(t) = (2\kappa + 3\alpha t)^2 + 2\Delta\,t^2$
(Definition~\ref{def:shadow-metric}) packages the dichotomy
geometrically: $Q_L$ is a perfect square iff
$\Delta = 0$, and the spectral curve
$\Sigma_L = \{H^2 = t^4 Q_L\}$
(Corollary~\ref{cor:spectral-curve}) is rational.

 & \S\ref{sec:en-koszul-duality} \\
\hline
\multicolumn{3}{l}{\textit{%
 April 2026 session: AP165 bar-complex operadic correction}} \\
\hline
\multicolumn{3}{p{13cm}}{%
 \textbf{AP165 correction (constitutional).}
 The bar complex $\barB(\cA)$ is an $E_1$ coassociative
 coalgebra over $(\mathrm{ChirAss})^!$, the Koszul dual
 cooperad of the chiral associative operad.
 It is \emph{not} an $\SCchtop$-coalgebra.
 The $\SCchtop$ structure emerges on the derived chiral
 center pair
 $(\cZ^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA),\; \cA)$:
 bulk acts on boundary.
 See Vol~II CLAUDE.md, AP165/B54--B56.} \\
\hline

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '7246,7268p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
 Koszul self-dual. The duality \emph{functor} is an
 involution; the \emph{operad} is not self-dual.
 See AP166/B57.} \\
\hline
\multicolumn{3}{p{13cm}}{%
 \textbf{Topologization scoped.}
 The cohomological topologization theorem
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:topologization}:
 $\SCchtop + \text{inner conformal vector}
 \Rightarrow E_3^{\mathrm{top}}$ on BRST cohomology)
 is proved for affine Kac--Moody at non-critical level.
 The same theorem gives an unconditional chain-level
 $E_3^{\mathrm{top}}$ model on the zero-differential
 cohomology complex, which is quasi-isomorphic as a chain
 complex to the original derived center. The lift to the
 original cochain complex is conditional on the
 $A_\infty$-coherence equation
 $[m,G]=\partial_z$; beyond affine Kac--Moody, the full
 package remains conjectural
 (Conjecture~\ref{conj:topologization-general}).} \\

 \hfill \checkmark
\item\label{item:kp-barcobar}
 Bar-cobar counit $\Omegach(\barBch(\cA)) \to \cA$ is a quasi-isomorphism
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}).
 \hfill \checkmark
\item\label{item:kp-barrbeck}
 Barr--Beck--Lurie comparison for $\barBch \dashv \Omegach$ is an equivalence
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:barr-beck-lurie-koszulness}).
 Conservativity + totalization on log-FM strata.
 \hfill \checkmark
\item\label{item:kp-facthom}
 Factorization homology $\textstyle\int_{\mathbb{P}^1} \cA$ in degree~$0$
 (the genus-$0$ specialization; higher genus only on the
 uniform-weight lane)
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:fh-concentration-koszulness}).
 Bar = FH; genus-$0$ concentration forces $E_2$-collapse.

\end{enumerate}

\medskip\noindent
 succeeded in 52ms:
\end{enumerate}

%% ------------------------------------------------------------------
\subsection{Sufficient conditions for chiral Koszulness}
\label{subsec:concordance-koszulness-sufficient}
\index{Koszul property!sufficient conditions|textbf}

Two sufficient conditions for chiral Koszulness are established:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
\item \emph{PBW universality}
 (Proposition~\ref{prop:pbw-universality}):
 freely strongly generated vertex algebras are chirally Koszul.
 Applies to the \emph{universal} algebras $V_k(\fg)$ (all $k$),
 $\mathrm{Vir}_c$ (all $c$), $\mathcal{W}^k(\fg)$ (Miura free
 generators). The simple quotient $L_k(\fg) = V_k(\fg)/I_k$
 is \emph{not} freely strongly generated when the null-vector ideal
 $I_k$ is nontrivial, and PBW universality does not apply to it.
\item \emph{Kac--Shapovalov criterion}
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:kac-shapovalov-koszulness}):
 $\det G_h \neq 0$ in the bar-relevant range.

+Proposition~\ref{prop:baxter-yangian-equivariance}; shifted-
+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.

I’ve reached the Koszul meta-theorem block. This is the last theorem surface that still compresses distinct claims together: item (vii) needs the genus-0/all-genera split made explicit, and item (viii) must stop suggesting the old stronger Hochschild package when the source now separates concentration/polynomial growth from any freeness upgrade.

/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "Massey|freeness|formality-hochschild|hochschild-polynomial-growth|fh-concentration-koszulness" chapters/theory/chiral_koszul_pairs.tex chapters/theory/chiral_hochschild_koszul.tex' in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
\begin{theorem}[Equivalences and consequences of chiral Koszulness; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{thm:koszul-equivalences-meta}
\index{Koszul property!equivalences of characterizations|textbf}

Let $\cA$ be a chiral algebra on a smooth projective curve~$X$
with PBW filtration $F_\bullet$.
Conditions \textup{(i)--(vi)}, \textup{(ix)--(x)}, and the
genus-$0$ clause of condition~\textup{(vii)} below are equivalent.
On the uniform-weight lane, condition~\textup{(vii)} strengthens to
the all-genera concentration statement recorded there and remains
equivalent to them.
Condition~\textup{(viii)} is a proved one-way consequence of
them on the Koszul locus: it yields Hochschild duality,
polynomial Hilbert series, and $\Etwo$-formality, but it does not
force the underlying graded-commutative algebra to be free.
Under the additional perfectness and non-degeneracy hypotheses on the
ambient tangent complex, condition~\textup{(xi)} is also equivalent to
them. Condition~\textup{(xii)} implies condition~\textup{(x)}
(Remark~\ref{rem:d-module-purity-content}); the converse is open.

+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.

thinking
**Exploring freeness conditions**

I’m thinking about the source theorem mentioning Etwo-formality and the Massey triviality. The user’s key update also states that freeness depends on the vanishing of the Massey product. I'm considering if there could be another clause that I need to find later. It seems like searching for freeness will help clarify things. I wonder how all these concepts connect and what implications they have for the theorem overall.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2160,2188p' chapters/theory/chiral_koszul_pairs.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
\smallskip\noindent
The quasi-isomorphism
$\Omega(\barBgeom(\cA)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \cA$ identifies the
bar-cobar resolution with the chiral Hochschild complex. On the
Koszul locus, Theorem~H,
Theorem~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-polynomial-growth}, and
Proposition~\ref{prop:e2-formality-hochschild} give the stated
cohomological concentration, duality, polynomial Hilbert series,
and $\Etwo$-formality. The proof of
Proposition~\ref{prop:e2-formality-hochschild} uses the formality of
the local configuration spaces $\FM_n(\mathbb{C})$, so the higher
braces and Massey products on $\ChirHoch^*(\cA)$ are
cohomologically trivial. No converse from
\textup{(viii)} to the bar-cobar counit is proved here.

\medskip
\textsc{$A_\infty$ formality}
\textup{(i)}$\Leftrightarrow$\textup{(iii)}:

+completion packet

+constraint supplied by
+Proposition~\ref{prop:baxter-yangian-equivariance}; shifted-
+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2260,2286p' chapters/theory/chiral_koszul_pairs.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
unconditional and is the exact equivalence used in the core circuit.
On the uniform-weight lane, Theorem~\ref{thm:loop-order-collapse}
and Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality} upgrade it to all genera:
factorization homology remains concentrated in degree~$0$, and its
scalar obstruction class is $\kappa(\cA)\cdot\lambda_g$
\textup{(UNIFORM-WEIGHT)}.
Off that lane the higher-genus scalar formula receives the
cross-channel correction of
Theorem~\ref{thm:multi-weight-genus-expansion}. Multi-weight
principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras therefore give the boundary
counterexamples: they are chirally Koszul on the genus-$0$ surface,
but at $g \geq 2$ one has
$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$, so the all-genera
strengthening of \textup{(vii)} fails. The sharpened statement is
therefore optimal.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[The strongest proved Hochschild consequence]
The strengthened form of \textup{(viii)} stops at duality,
polynomial Hilbert series, and $\Etwo$-formality. It does not imply

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2286,2318p' chapters/theory/chiral_koszul_pairs.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
The degree-$2$ class squares to zero because
$\ChirHoch^4(\mathrm{Vir}_c)=0$, whereas a free
graded-commutative algebra on a degree-$2$ generator would have
nonzero degree~$4$. Thus vanishing of higher braces and Massey
products is the strongest proved consequence; ordinary cup-product
relations may remain.
\end{remark}

\medskip
\textsc{FM boundary acyclicity}
\textup{(i)}$\Leftrightarrow$\textup{(x)}:

\smallskip\noindent
\textup{(i)}$\Rightarrow$\textup{(x)}:
Each FM boundary stratum $S_T \cong \prod_{v \in V(T)}
\overline{\operatorname{Conf}}_{|v|}(X_v)$ is indexed by a tree~$T$.
The residue component $d_{\mathrm{res}}|_{S_T}$ of the bar
differential restricts to the tensor product of lower-degree bar
complexes. $E_2$-collapse at each vertex degree
(Theorem~\ref{thm:pbw-koszulness-criterion}) gives acyclicity:

(ii)~factorization compatibility of the bar-cobar counit;
(iii)~conservative assembly via
Theorem~\ref{thm:stratified-conservative-restriction}.
This closes structural gap~H1 and completes Future~2.

\subsection{The Lagrangian form of complementarity}

Theorem~C (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}) establishes
the direct-sum decomposition
$Q_g(\cA) \oplus Q_g(\cA^!)
= H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, \mathcal{Z}(\cA))$

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '7764,7774p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
The bar complex $\barBch(\cA)$ is an algebra over the Feynman
transform of the commutative modular operad, with total
differential $D_\cA$ satisfying $D_\cA^2 = 0$ at all genera and
degrees
(Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-modular-operad},
Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-diff-squares-zero}).
This is the foundational consistency: the universal MC element
$\Theta_\cA := D_\cA - d_0$ exists in $\MC(\fg^{\mathrm{mod}}_\cA)$
precisely because $D_\cA^2 = 0$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}).
The test is algebraic: it holds for every chiral algebra satisfying

+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),

+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a

+\paragraph{MC3 after the type-$A$ reduction.}
+Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types} still records the two unconditional
+all-types layers: categorical prefundamental Clebsch--Gordan and the
+DK-2/3 comparison on the evaluation-generated core.
+The new load-bearing theorem is
+Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution}: in type~$A$ the old
+four-packet post-core problem is reduced to the single compact-
+completion packet
+Conjecture~\ref{conj:dk-compacts-completion}. The proved post-core
+surface is the Baxter-locus seed
+$\{V_{\omega_i}(a)\}\cup\{L^-_{i,a-\frac{1}{2}}\}$, with the spectral
+constraint supplied by
+Proposition~\ref{prop:baxter-yangian-equivariance}; shifted-
+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.
 
 
 \paragraph{Holographic central charge identification (conjectural).}

thinking
**Planning patch updates**

I'm looking at line 7768, and it seems there may not be an issue there, but I need to summarize patches around line 4708 with strong statuses. For line 6808, which mentions "by Theorem H," I think it could work but might be ambiguous. I'm thinking of patching key sections like kp item vii/viii, proof circuit/status, and the MC status table row, as well as focusing on the modular summary and possibly a sentence in volume 2. I need to refine these modifications.
thinking
**Updating kp-facthom item**

I'm working on the kp-facthom item. The current description mentions factorization homology for the genus-0 specialization, while the proposed change refines it to emphasize that it's concentrated in degree 0 on the uniform-weight lane. It also includes how the upgrade affects all genera and addresses boundary counterexamples for g≥2. I want to ensure that the unconditional check mark is kept since that applies only to genus-0, while noting that the all-genera upgrade is indeed proven. I think this adjustment works.
thinking
**Updating kp-hochschild item**

I'm reviewing the kp-hochschild item and considering changes. The current text explains that $\ChirHoch^*$ is concentrated and satisfies duality. The proposed update will highlight that it has a polynomial Hilbert series of degree ≤2 and is E_2-formal on the Koszul locus. I'm careful to avoid a blanket freeness claim, since the boundary counterexample shows that Vir generic provides a case where the degree-2 class squares to zero. I think this update maintains clarity.
thinking
**Considering topologization and summaries**

I think topologization could be okay, but I'm not completely sure about how to phrase "chain-level via qi model" since it seems to be already in place. I should also check for other summary lines, like those starting at 11207, that might include MC3's old rank-independence. It feels important to finalize this patch after ensuring it’s completely fresh, so I'll approach crafting that patch with care and attention.
codex
I’m making the final structural patch now: the Koszul meta-theorem summary, the proof circuit, and the modular status table. This is the last place where a compressed summary can accidentally undo the stronger scoped statements already fixed above.

For the $\mathcal{W}_3$ PVA, $\mathrm{Mod}(V) = 0$
(Volume~II, Theorem~N). The lift freedom is one-dimensional,
generated by the central-parameter direction: the unique
deformation that produces the quantized $\mathcal{W}_3$
at central charge $c = c(k)$. This connects Volume~II's
PVA descent (Theorem~G) with Volume~I's shadow obstruction tower
(the degree-$2$ projection $\Theta_\cA^{\leq 2}$ recovers
the modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$, which is the
quantized value of the classical variational class).
\end{remark}


\section[Towards modular homotopy theory]{Towards modular homotopy theory for factorization algebras on curves}
\label{sec:modular-koszul-programme}
\index{modular Koszul duality!project}

The modular characteristic theorem (Theorem~D,
Theorem~\ref{thm:modular-characteristic}) establishes the scalar
logarithmic datum~$\kappa(\cA)$. This section identifies the six
components (five structural extensions and the Hochschild
file update:

\section[Towards modular homotopy theory]{Towards modular homotopy theory for factorization algebras on curves}
\label{sec:modular-koszul-programme}
\index{modular Koszul duality!project}

The modular characteristic theorem (Theorem~D,
Theorem~\ref{thm:modular-characteristic}) establishes the scalar
logarithmic datum~$\kappa(\cA)$. This section identifies the six
components (five structural extensions and the Hochschild
cohomology theorem) needed to pass from the proved modular Koszul
core to modular homotopy theory for factorization algebras on
curves; that is, to complete the nilpotence-periodicity
correspondence
(Remark~\ref{rem:nilpotence-periodicity}) from the scalar level to
the full categorical level:

\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.3}
\begin{tabular}{@{}cp{4.4cm}p{3.8cm}p{4.6cm}@{}}
\toprule
\textbf{Label} & \textbf{Statement} & \textbf{Status} & \textbf{Reference} \\

+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that

\begin{remark}[Four levels of modular invariant]%
\label{rem:four-levels}%
\index{modular homotopy theory!four levels}%
The six components above implement a hierarchy of modular
invariants of increasing categorical depth.
The proved scalar level is the lowest;
the full modular homotopy type is the highest:

+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),

+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved consequence on the Koszul locus, the Lagrangian criterion
+~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is

-\paragraph{MC3 three-layer split and Lemma~L.}

-core, all simple types, unconditional), MC3b (categorical

-category~$\cO$, type~A unconditional, other types conditional

+\paragraph{MC3 after the type-$A$ reduction.}
+Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types} still records the two unconditional
+all-types layers: categorical prefundamental Clebsch--Gordan and the
+DK-2/3 comparison on the evaluation-generated core.
+The new load-bearing theorem is
+Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution}: in type~$A$ the old
+four-packet post-core problem is reduced to the single compact-
+completion packet
+Conjecture~\ref{conj:dk-compacts-completion}. The proved post-core
+surface is the Baxter-locus seed
+$\{V_{\omega_i}(a)\}\cup\{L^-_{i,a-\frac{1}{2}}\}$, with the spectral
+constraint supplied by
+Proposition~\ref{prop:baxter-yangian-equivariance}; shifted-
+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.

+\emph{Proof web.}
+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.
+The shortest loop is
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),
+and
+(\ref{item:kp-facthom})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-pbw})
+bypass the old linear spine; the Ext, Kac--Shapovalov,
+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

 $d_{\mathrm{gen}} = 3$ (finite), $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = \infty$
 (class~M). Bare $d(\cA)$ without subscript is forbidden.

-Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types} is rewritten as an
-explicit three-layer decomposition: MC3a (evaluation-generated
-core, all simple types, unconditional), MC3b (categorical
-thick generation, all types, unconditional), MC3c (full
-category~$\cO$, type~A unconditional, other types conditional

+\paragraph{MC3 after the type-$A$ reduction.}
+Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types} still records the two unconditional
+all-types layers: categorical prefundamental Clebsch--Gordan and the
+DK-2/3 comparison on the evaluation-generated core.
+The new load-bearing theorem is
+Theorem~\ref{thm:mc3-type-a-resolution}: in type~$A$ the old
+four-packet post-core problem is reduced to the single compact-
+completion packet
+Conjecture~\ref{conj:dk-compacts-completion}. The proved post-core
+surface is the Baxter-locus seed
+$\{V_{\omega_i}(a)\}\cup\{L^-_{i,a-\frac{1}{2}}\}$, with the spectral
+constraint supplied by
+Proposition~\ref{prop:baxter-yangian-equivariance}; shifted-
+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.
 
 
 \paragraph{Holographic central charge identification (conjectural).}

+The proved core on

+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.

+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),

+bypass the old linear spine; the Ext, Kac--Shapovalov,
+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from

+\emph{Proof web.}
+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.
+The shortest loop is
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),
+and
+(\ref{item:kp-facthom})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-pbw})
+bypass the old linear spine; the Ext, Kac--Shapovalov,
+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

+completion packet
+Conjecture~\ref{conj:dk-compacts-completion}. The proved post-core

+constraint supplied by

+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.

+\emph{Proof web.}
+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.
+The shortest loop is
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),
+and
+(\ref{item:kp-facthom})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-pbw})
+bypass the old linear spine; the Ext, Kac--Shapovalov,
+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

+Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types} still records the two unconditional

+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.

+\emph{Proof web.}
+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.
+The shortest loop is
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),

+bypass the old linear spine; the Ext, Kac--Shapovalov,
+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '3296,3344p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
stronger all-genera claim through
$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
in general, and the
$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
one-directional.


%% ====================================================================
%% RECTIFICATION: THE ORGANIZING META-THEOREM
%% ====================================================================
\section{Rectification: the organizing meta-theorem}
\label{sec:concordance-rectification}
\index{rectification!organizing meta-theorem|textbf}

/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n -F '\\Omega/z' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[PVA quantization bridge]
\label{rem:concordance-pva-bridge}
\index{Poisson vertex algebra!quantization bridge}%
Volume~II constructs the modular PVA quantization pipeline:
a Poisson vertex algebra $V$ (the classical shadow of a
chiral algebra) determines a stable-graph modular deformation
complex $\mathrm{Def}_{\mathrm{cyc}}^{\mathrm{mod}}(V)$,
and the genus-$1$ obstruction is the variational modular class
$\mathrm{Mod}(V) = [\Delta_{\mathrm{cyc}} P_\Pi]$.
For the $\mathcal{W}_3$ PVA, $\mathrm{Mod}(V) = 0$
(Volume~II, Theorem~N). The lift freedom is one-dimensional,
generated by the central-parameter direction: the unique
deformation that produces the quantized $\mathcal{W}_3$
at central charge $c = c(k)$. This connects Volume~II's
PVA descent (Theorem~G) with Volume~I's shadow obstruction tower
(the degree-$2$ projection $\Theta_\cA^{\leq 2}$ recovers
the modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$, which is the
quantized value of the classical variational class).

+\emph{Proof web.}
+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.
+The shortest loop is
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),

+(\ref{item:kp-facthom})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-pbw})
+bypass the old linear spine; the Ext, Kac--Shapovalov,
+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '4590,4610p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobarexec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '4760,4792p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
a Poisson vertex algebra $V$ (the classical shadow of a
chiral algebra) determines a stable-graph modular deformation
complex $\mathrm{Def}_{\mathrm{cyc}}^{\mathrm{mod}}(V)$,
and the genus-$1$ obstruction is the variational modular class
$\mathrm{Mod}(V) = [\Delta_{\mathrm{cyc}} P_\Pi]$.
For the $\mathcal{W}_3$ PVA, $\mathrm{Mod}(V) = 0$
(Volume~II, Theorem~N). The lift freedom is one-dimensional,
generated by the central-parameter direction: the unique
deformation that produces the quantized $\mathcal{W}_3$
at central charge $c = c(k)$. This connects Volume~II's
PVA descent (Theorem~G) with Volume~I's shadow obstruction tower
(the degree-$2$ projection $\Theta_\cA^{\leq 2}$ recovers
the modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$, which is the
quantized value of the classical variational class).
\end{remark}


\section[Towards modular homotopy theory]{Towards modular homotopy theory for factorization algebras on curves}
\label{sec:modular-koszul-programme}
\index{modular Koszul duality!project}
 succeeded in 51ms:
 $c_{\mathrm{matter}} + c_{\mathrm{Liouville}} +
 c_{\mathrm{ghost}} = 0$
 is the ambient complementarity theorem
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}).
 \hfill\textup{(Structural)}
\item \emph{Polyakov connection on Teichm\"uller space.}
 The shadow connection~$\nabla^{\mathrm{sh}}$ should define a
 flat connection on the Teichm\"uller space~$\mathcal{T}_g$,
 with monodromy encoding the mapping-class group
 representation.
 \hfill\textup{(To be developed)}
\end{enumerate}

\paragraph{Bibliographic pointers.}
Polyakov~1981 (Polyakov action),
Belavin--Polyakov--Zamolodchikov~1984 (conformal symmetry),
Belavin--Knizhnik~1986 (factorization on~$\overline{\cM}_g$),
Alvarez~1983 (Polyakov--Alvarez formula).

\paragraph{Cross-references.}

+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})

+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),

+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

-Ext diagonal vanishing~(\ref{item:kp-ext}) follows by bigrading

+\emph{Proof web.}
+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.
+The shortest loop is
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),
+and
+(\ref{item:kp-facthom})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-pbw})
+bypass the old linear spine; the Ext, Kac--Shapovalov,
+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

+prefundamental generation and pro-Weyl recovery are proved on that
+surface. The remaining completion step is therefore a Baxter-
+constrained compact-completion problem, not an independent
+rank-induction packet.

 \end{center}

 succeeded in 51ms:
genus-$1{+}$ vertex weights use
$\ell_2^{(1)} = S_3\kappa/24 - S_3^2$ (exact from MC; Beilinson audit

\paragraph{Chiral Hochschild descent lemma.}
Lemma~\ref{lem:chirhoch-descent} records an explicit step
in the proof of Theorem~H: the chiral Hochschild complex
$\ChirHoch^{\bullet}(\cA)$ arises as the $\Sigma$-coinvariant
descent of the bar complex along Verdier intertwining,
$\ChirHoch^{\bullet}(\cA) \cong
(\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\barB(\cA))_{\Sigma}$,
on the Koszul locus. This identification was implicit in
the original Theorem~H proof; the lemma makes it citable

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '11198,11218p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobarexec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '4586,4602p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
 Proved for Heisenberg and lattice VOAs; open in general.
\end{enumerate}
Items~(i) and~(iii) are identification problems: which cohomology
class does a given projection of $\Theta^{\mathrm{oc}}_\cA$ produce?
Item~(ii) is a functoriality problem: is $\Theta^{\mathrm{oc}}_\cA$
the image of $\Theta^{\mathrm{BV}}_T$ under a comparison functor?
Items~(iv) and~(v) are extension problems: does the MC element
carry information beyond the formal/algebraic setting?
\end{remark}

% ======================================================================
\section{Forbidden slogans}
\label{sec:concordance-forbidden-slogans}
\index{forbidden slogans}
% ======================================================================

The following statements are false.
They must never appear in this monograph.
Each violates a specific anti-pattern documented
in the Beilinson Principle
 succeeded in 53ms:
 \hfill\textup{(Conjectural)}
\item \emph{Non-critical string theory as Liouville $+$
 complementarity.}
 The anomaly cancellation
 $c_{\mathrm{matter}} + c_{\mathrm{Liouville}} +
 c_{\mathrm{ghost}} = 0$
 is the ambient complementarity theorem
 (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}).
 \hfill\textup{(Structural)}
\item \emph{Polyakov connection on Teichm\"uller space.}
 The shadow connection~$\nabla^{\mathrm{sh}}$ should define a
 flat connection on the Teichm\"uller space~$\mathcal{T}_g$,
 with monodromy encoding the mapping-class group
 representation.
 \hfill\textup{(To be developed)}
\end{enumerate}

+\emph{Proof web.}
+The proved core on
+(\ref{item:kp-def})--(\ref{item:kp-facthom}),
+(\ref{item:kp-nullvec}), (\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+is a web, not a single chain.
+The shortest loop is
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-def})
+via the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms.
+Independent direct arrows
+(\ref{item:kp-def})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}),
+(\ref{item:kp-ainfty})$\Rightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy}),
+(\ref{item:kp-barcobar})$\Leftrightarrow$(\ref{item:kp-barrbeck}),

+and FM-boundary criteria
+(\ref{item:kp-ext}), (\ref{item:kp-nullvec}),
+(\ref{item:kp-fmbdy})
+each force PBW collapse directly.
+Hence no single proved implication disconnects the unconditional
+core.
+The factorization-homology arrow is genus-$0$ in general and
+upgrades to all genera only on the uniform-weight lane; multi-weight
+principal $\mathcal{W}$-algebras witness the boundary failure of the
+stronger all-genera claim through
+$\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}} \neq 0$.
+The Hochschild statement~(\ref{item:kp-hochschild}) remains a
+proved one-way consequence on the Koszul locus: it is obtained from
+bar-cobar inversion via Theorem~H, polynomial growth, and
+$\Etwo$-formality, with no converse back to the bar-cobar counit.
+The Lagrangian criterion~(\ref{item:kp-lagrangian}) is conditional
+in general, and the
+$\mathcal{D}$-module purity statement~(\ref{item:kp-dmod}) is
+one-directional.

Theorem~\ref{thm:ran-coherent-bar-cobar} resolves the Ran-space
formality gap identified in earlier audits: the bar-cobar
quasi-isomorphism extends coherently from
$\operatorname{Ran}^{\leq n}(X)$ to all of
$\operatorname{Ran}(X)$ via the strong completion-tower theorem
and the degree cutoff lemma.

\paragraph{Ambient complementarity FMP upgrade.}
Theorem~\ref{thm:ambient-complementarity-fmp} is upgraded from
\texttt{Conjectured} to \texttt{ProvedHere}: the modular cyclic
deformation complex $\Defcyc^{\mathrm{mod}}(\cA)$ is already a
complete filtered cyclic dg~Lie algebra, and the Lurie--Pridham
correspondence + PTVV theorem give the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic
formal moduli problem with Lagrangian maps unconditionally (under
the standing perfectness/nondegeneracy hypotheses).

\paragraph{Chiral Deligne--Tamarkin principle and the dimensional jump.}
 succeeded in 51ms:
 Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:ambient-complementarity-tangent}(iii)},
 the ambient complementarity formal moduli problem
 $\mathcal{M}_{\mathrm{comp}}(\cA)$
 carries a proved $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure, and
 $\mathcal{M}_{\cA}$ and $\mathcal{M}_{\cA^!}$ define
 complementary Lagrangian maps
 \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:ambient-complementarity-fmp}}\textup{)}.
\end{enumerate}
\end{theorem}

\begin{remark}[Degree clarification]\label{rem:lagrangian-conj-scope}
The original formulation of this result stated a single
$(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on~$C_g$. The proved version
exec

The monograph develops both axes: the oper package of
Chapter~\ref{chap:kac-moody} and the critical-level
Hochschild computation of
Theorem~\ref{thm:critical-level-cohomology} belong to
axis~1; the shadow obstruction tower, the genus expansion, and
Theorems~A--D belong to axis~2.
Theorem~H (chiral Hochschild) connects them: at generic level,
$\ChirHoch^n = 0$ for $n > 2$ with $P(t) = 1 + t^2$
(concentration from the de~Rham bound on a curve);
the continuous Lie algebra cohomology
$H^*_{\mathrm{cont}} = \mathbb{C}[\Theta_1, \ldots, \Theta_r]$
 succeeded in 52ms:
$Z_{\mathrm{der}}(\cA^!)$, which identifies with the chiral
Hochschild cochain complex $\operatorname{HH}^*_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA^!)$
via the corrected Koszul triangle
(Vol~II, Theorem~J): $A_{\mathrm{bulk}} \simeq
Z_{\mathrm{der}}(B_\partial) \simeq
\operatorname{HH}^*_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA^!)$,
where $B_\partial := \cA^!$ is the boundary algebra.
This recovers Theorem~H
(Theorems~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch},
\ref{thm:hochschild-polynomial-growth}) via dimensional
descent.
\end{conjecture}

 succeeded in 51ms:
 genuswise chain-level BV/BRST/bar identification remains
 conjectural (Conjecture~\ref{conj:v1-master-bv-brst};
 class~M chain-level false).
 MC3 splits into three layers
 (Corollary~\ref{cor:mc3-all-types}):
 MC3a/b unconditional for all simple types;
 MC3c (full Cat~$\cO$) unconditional in type~A,
 conditional on Conjecture~\ref{conj:rank-independence-step2}
 (Lemma~L) in other types.
 The broader programme contains open problems downstream
 of MC3: the DK-4/5 extension/completion packet
 (Conjecture~\ref{conj:dk-compacts-completion}),
 multi-generator universality at $g \geq 2$
 (Open Problem~\ref{op:multi-generator-universality}, now
 resolved negatively: cross-channel correction nonzero),
 succeeded in 52ms:
H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, Z(\cA))$ (complementarity).}
The factorization homology of the Koszul pair $(\cA, \cA^!)$
assembles into a Lagrangian decomposition of the shifted-symplectic
target
(Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main},
Theorem~\ref{thm:ambient-complementarity-fmp}).
This is the only test that involves both $\cA$ and its Koszul
dual $\cA^!$ simultaneously. It controls the anomaly-cancellation
structure: the total genus-$g$ partition function of a physical
system with matter~$\cA$ and ghosts~$\cA^!$ is a topological
invariant precisely when complementarity holds.

thinking
**Addressing item discrepancies**

I need to patch some stale items. For instance, the 3976 paragraph about the ambient complementarity FMP upgrade states it was upgraded unconditionally under certain hypotheses, but the user wanted it conditional on uniform-weight. Oh, and I should check if the corresponding theorem is now proved. I’ll inspect other lines, like 5078 and 7839, that reference this theorem, to ensure everything is consistent and synced with the source since I’m only editing the concordance file.
codex
I found one more substantive drift surface: the concordance still contains an internal “ambient complementarity FMP” theorem package that advertises a proved unconditional upgrade. That conflicts with the current Theorem C2 lane, which is conditional on the uniform-weight/BV package. I’m downgrading that internal concordance restatement to match the live theorem source instead of leaving a hidden contradiction inside the constitution.

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '5048,5096p' chapters/connections/concordance.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
\subsection{The Lagrangian form of complementarity}

Theorem~C (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}) establishes
the direct-sum decomposition
$Q_g(\cA) \oplus Q_g(\cA^!)
= H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, \mathcal{Z}(\cA))$
via the eigenspace decomposition of the Verdier involution.
The natural strengthening is a Lagrangian-polarization theorem,
now proved in two layers:

\begin{theorem}[Lagrangian complementarity;
\ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:lagrangian-complementarity}
\index{Lagrangian!complementarity|textbf}
For a modular Koszul pair $(\cA, \cA^!)$, the following hold:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item the complex
 $C_g := R\Gamma(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, \mathcal{Z}_{\cA})$
 carries the proved Verdier-side
 $(-(3g{-}3))$-shifted symplectic structure of
 Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:ptvv-lagrangian}}, and
 succeeded in 52ms:
\smallskip
\noindent\textbf{Test~2: $\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA) = \kappa(\cA)\cdot\lambda_g$
(obstruction = modular characteristic $\times$ Hodge class).}
The genus-$g$ obstruction class in $H^{2g}(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g)$
factors as a universal algebra-dependent scalar $\kappa(\cA)$ times
the Faber--Pandharipande Hodge integral $\lambda_g$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:modular-characteristic}).
This is proved unconditionally at genus~$1$ for all modular Koszul
algebras, and at all genera on the uniform-weight lane
(Theorem~\ref{thm:algebraic-family-rigidity}).
The test is tautological-geometric: it asserts that the bar complex
produces the Hodge class $\lambda_g$, not an arbitrary class in
$H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g)$.

\smallskip
\noindent\textbf{Test~3: $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) =
H^*(\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g, Z(\cA))$ (complementarity).}
The factorization homology of the Koszul pair $(\cA, \cA^!)$
assembles into a Lagrangian decomposition of the shifted-symplectic
target
 succeeded in 52ms:
2068:\item The \emph{ambient complementarity upgrade}
3976:Theorem~\ref{thm:ambient-complementarity-fmp} is upgraded from
4592: is the ambient complementarity theorem
5073: the ambient complementarity formal moduli problem
5078: \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:ambient-complementarity-fmp}}\textup{)}.
7839:Theorem~\ref{thm:ambient-complementarity-fmp}).
